title: Dense Ethiopian Lentil Stew
description: A tangy and dense one-pot Ethiopian dish featuring grana-padano and kombu-cold-extract, this stew is a halal-friendly option for a hearty dinner. It combines the flavors of lentils, onions, and spices with the richness of grana-padano cheese.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is softened and lightly browned.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Add the red lentils, kombu-cold-extract mixture, and berbere spice, and stir to combine.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 30 minutes.
Stir in the crumbled grana-padano cheese until it is melted and the stew is heated through.
Season the stew with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh herbs if desired.
